component.name: Box
component.description: The foundational layout primitive. A polymorphic container that applies spacing tokens via the padding props and controls overflow clipping. Everything else in the system can be placed inside a Box.

when: Wrapping content that needs padding without imposing flex or grid layout
Providing semantic HTML structure via the as prop (section, article, aside, main)
Creating a padded surface or card-like region before Card is available

patterns.name: padded-surface
patterns.description: A Box wrapping a Stack to create a padded vertical content region.

patterns.name: page-main
patterns.description: Box rendered as the page's <main> landmark — the polymorphic as prop emits a real <main> element. Exactly one per route (ADR-011).

patterns.name: page-header
patterns.description: Box rendered as the page's <header> landmark. Use AppHeader directly when the full header component is available; any child <nav> needs a unique aria-label.

patterns.name: page-section
patterns.description: Box rendered as a <section> landmark. aria-labelledby must point to the visible Heading id; use aria-label only when there is no visible heading. Add paddingY for vertical rhythm. A .container div inside handles max-width and grid margins.

patterns.name: page-footer
patterns.description: Box rendered as the page's <footer> (contentinfo landmark). One per page; a .container div inside handles max-width and grid margins.

patterns.name: container
patterns.description: Box with className='container' to center content in a section. The .container global class applies max-width + grid margin (packages/components/src/styles/grid.css). Prefer this over a raw <div className='container'>.

antiPatterns.scenario: Using Box to apply flex or grid layout
antiPatterns.reason: Box has no layout opinions beyond padding. It is not a flex container.
antiPatterns.alternative: Use Stack for vertical flex layout or Inline for horizontal flex layout

antiPatterns.scenario: Nesting Boxes purely to add padding when a single Box with paddingX/paddingY suffices
antiPatterns.reason: Unnecessary nesting inflates the DOM and makes spacing harder to reason about
antiPatterns.alternative: Use the paddingX and paddingY props on a single Box to apply asymmetric spacing

antiPatterns.scenario: Using a raw <div className='container'> instead of Box
antiPatterns.reason: The grammar requires all structural elements to go through the component system. A raw div bypasses the as-prop pattern and makes the tree inconsistent.
antiPatterns.alternative: Use <Box className='container'> or <div className='container'> only as a presentational wrapper inside a Box landmark — never as a replacement for a landmark Box

antiPatterns.scenario: Setting color via inline style on Box or a child span
antiPatterns.reason: Inline color styles bypass the token system and break dark-mode switching.
antiPatterns.alternative: Use <Text color='…'> or <Heading> with a token-backed color prop. Apply the semantic color token on the element that owns the color context, not on Box.

antiPatterns.scenario: Hand-writing style={{ flex: '1 0 0' }}, style={{ minWidth }}, style={{ maxWidth }}, style={{ minHeight }}, or style={{ maxHeight }} on a Box for column fill or size constraints
antiPatterns.reason: Box has first-class grow/minWidth/maxWidth/minHeight/maxHeight props that emit the same CSS through the component's own style resolution — inline style duplicates this and drifts from it over time.
antiPatterns.alternative: Use the grow prop (flex: n 0 0) for column fill, and minWidth/maxWidth/minHeight/maxHeight props for size constraints, instead of an inline style object.

purpose.default: A generic padded container. Rendered as div by default; override with the as prop for semantic HTML.
Flat surface on the page background — same fill as Card's default variant, for a Box standing in as a plain surface.
purpose.inverted: Dark surface for CTA banners, footers, or other sections that invert against the page's default theme.
purpose.transparent: Explicitly no fill — lets the parent surface show through.
